input:
"#CONTEXT#
You are a B2B sales strategist writing a GTM intelligence memo for a sales team. You will receive information about:
1. A client company whose product is being sold
2. A lead who previously worked at a company that is a known customer of the client
3. The lead's current company where they now hold a relevant role

The lead's past company is a known customer of the client. The lead held a relevant role there and would have been exposed to or aware of the client's product. They have since moved to a new company in a similar or more senior role."

"#INPUTS#
client_company_name: Nostra
client_company_domain: nostra.ai
client_company_description: Nostra helps the world’s leading ecommerce brands future-proof their digital infrastructure with cutting-edge edge technology. We enable high-growth DTC companies to deploy enterprise-grade infrastructure in minutes, not months. Our edge platform is designed to accelerate site speed, increase conversion rates, and drive more revenue, while protecting your brand from modern threats. With products like Edge Delivery Engine, Edge Protection, Edge ID, and Edge Crawler, we give brands the tools to: • Boost performance by 20–30% • Block ad bot traffic before it hits your site • Extend cookie life for better attribution • Optimize content for LLM indexing and AI-native search Brands like Jolie, Amiri, and Olukai rely on Nostra to deliver faster, safer, smarter ecommerce experiences

lead_full_name: 
lead_linkedin_url: Edward Zenon
lead_current_job_title: https://www.linkedin.com/in/edward-zenon-3b9b8850
lead_current_company_name: CEO/ Designer
lead_current_company_domain: Jupiter Fashion Agency
lead_current_company_description: universalweather.com

lead_past_company_name: At Universal, we’re moving organizations that move the world. 

As the #1 mission management company for private flight departments, we’re supporting hundreds of flights daily that touch peoples’ lives in all kinds of way – including business, government, leisure, family, humanitarian and more. 

We’re continually growing and adapting to the ever-changes needs of our customers and industry, and we’re innovating new services and technologies that will propel private aviation in the future. 

We love our customers, we love people, and we’re always on the hunt for talented individuals to join our team. 

For a listing of our current career opportunities, please visit www.universalweather.com/careers.

 Voted Houston Chronicle's Top Workplaces 2021.
lead_past_company_domain: Forever 21
lead_past_company_description: forever21.com
lead_past_company_job_title: Forever 21 continues to be a fashion industry leader with a mission to make the latest trends accessible to all. F21 persists on staying ahead of innovation and providing styles and fit that our customers love. While reinventing for now and beyond, we’re seeking passionate and creative new family members to join us on this adventure! F21 continues to operate E-commerce and 450 locations across the U.S. along with maintaining a strong brand DNA with consumers and within the fashion industry. 

In February 2020, Forever 21 came under new ownership made up of Simon Property Group, Brookfield Property Partners, and Authentic Brands Group. Under this new ownership F21 has the financial strength and long term view that will prepare us for the future, while continuing to set the same high standards as it always has. Join our team and learn the inner sanctum of the global fashion industry, while we build the new Forever 21! 

Forever 21 is operated by SPARC Group LLC.
 
About SPARC Group LLC
SPARC Group LLC offers operational and strategic expertise including retail and e-commerce management, sourcing, design and technology for leading global brands. SPARC Group is a joint venture between Simon Property Group, a preeminent retail real estate company and Authentic Brands Group, a global leader in brand development. In addition to Forever 21, SPARC Group brands include Aéropostale, Brooks Brothers, Nautica, Eddie Bauer, and Lucky Brand.
 
SPARC Group LLC is an equal opportunity employer, and is committed to maintaining a workplace free from prohibited employment conduct, including discrimination or harassment on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, religion, disability, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, domestic partner status, civil partnership, status as a covered veteran, status in the Uniformed Services of the United States, citizenship and any other characteristic protected by law."

"#OBJECTIVE#
Generate a 5-section GTM strategy memo that helps a sales rep understand why this lead is a high-value target right now and exactly how to approach them.

#RESEARCH DIRECTIVES#
Before writing the memo, research the following:

1. **Current company business context** — What is the current company's scale, market position, ecommerce footprint, and growth trajectory? What operational or technical challenges would a company like this face? What about their stage creates urgency for the client's product?

2. **Competitive landscape** — Who are the current company's direct competitors? What market pressure do they face? How does site performance, conversion optimization, or digital infrastructure factor into their competitive position?

3. **Lead's role and authority** — Based on their title and the company context, what does this person own? What decisions do they influence or control? Why are they the right person to talk to about the client's product?

4. **The alumni signal** — The lead previously held a relevant role at a company that uses the client's product. What would they have seen or experienced there? How does that experience translate to their current situation?

5. **Complexity delta** — How do the challenges at the current company compare to the past company? Are they dealing with more brands, more SKUs, more markets, more traffic, more complexity? Frame the current situation relative to what they've already navigated.

#OUTPUT FORMAT#
Write a 5-section memo in sharp, conversational prose. No bullet points. No headers beyond the section names. Each section should be a tight paragraph.

**OVERVIEW**
2-3 sentences. Who is this person, what is their current role, and what is their connection to the client's customer base. State the alumni signal clearly.

**MARKET POSITION**
One paragraph. The current company's business context — scale, growth stage, competitive pressure, and why their digital infrastructure matters right now. What is at stake for them?

**RELEVANCE & READINESS**
One paragraph. Why this specific person. What did they see or own at their past company? What do they own now? Why would they recognize the value of the client's product immediately?

**RECOMMENDED APPROACH**
One paragraph. The specific angle for outreach. Include a sample message or talk track that references the alumni connection and speaks to the current company's situation. Make it concrete, not generic.

**TAKEAWAY**
One sentence. The verdict — why this lead matters and the urgency level.

#STYLE GUIDANCE#
- Be direct and specific. No filler, no hedge words.
- Do not fabricate metrics, quotes, or claims. Only infer plausibly from the inputs and your research.
- Do not claim the lead directly used or implemented the client's product unless you have evidence. Frame it as exposure and awareness based on their role.
- The memo should read like it was written by someone who understands the market, not by an AI summarizing inputs.
- Total length: 300-400 words.

Output Schema
json{
  ""overview"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""2-3 sentences. Who is this person, their current role, and their alumni connection to the client's customer base.""
  },
  ""market_position"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph on the current company's business context, competitive pressure, and why digital infrastructure matters now.""
  },
  ""relevance_and_readiness"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph on why this specific person — what they saw at the past company, what they own now, why they'd recognize value immediately.""
  },
  ""recommended_approach"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph with the specific outreach angle and sample messaging that references the alumni connection.""
  },
  ""takeaway"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One sentence verdict on why this lead matters and urgency level.""
  }
}"

overview.lead_identity: Edward Zenon is the CEO/Designer and co-founder of Jupiter Fashion Agency, a Houston-based fashion brand he has led since 2008. Concurrently, he holds the position of Global Regulatory Specialist at Universal Weather and Aviation.
overview.alumni_signal: Mr. Zenon previously served as a Manager of Service and Training at Forever 21 from 2013 to 2016. Forever 21 is a prominent, published customer of Nostra, with a success story detailing significant improvements in website performance, including a 59% increase in page load speed and a 166% faster crawl speed, directly attributable to Nostra's solutions.

market_position.company_context: Jupiter Fashion Agency presents as a boutique, founder-led fashion label and creative agency. Its limited public web presence, primarily validated through social media and the founder's profile, suggests a lean operational scale. The business model likely relies on social media for demand generation, direct-to-consumer sales, and potentially wholesale channels, where brand storytelling and product drops are key.
market_position.competitive_pressure: In the crowded fashion market, Jupiter Fashion Agency competes against highly optimized direct-to-consumer brands and large marketplaces. These competitors invest heavily in technology to ensure fast, seamless mobile shopping experiences. For a smaller brand, this creates immense pressure to deliver a comparable or superior user experience to capture and convert customers effectively.
market_position.infrastructure_urgency: For a brand of this scale, digital infrastructure is a critical lever for growth. Mobile-first site speed, efficient SEO crawlability for organic discovery, and protection from bot traffic that wastes ad spend are not luxuries but necessities. Every millisecond of page load delay can lead to lost sales, and every dollar wasted on bot traffic erodes thin margins. Therefore, implementing enterprise-grade performance infrastructure is an urgent priority to maximize conversion rates, improve ad efficiency, and secure a competitive footing.

relevance_and_readiness.past_experience: During his tenure as a manager at Forever 21, a high-velocity fast-fashion retailer, Edward Zenon would have been proximate to the operational challenges where site performance directly impacts sales. He would have been aware of the constant pressure to optimize the customer journey and the significant business cost of slow page loads and poor site availability, the very issues Nostra was brought in to solve, ultimately cutting TTFB by 53% and boosting page speeds by 59% for the retailer.
relevance_and_readiness.current_authority: As the CEO, co-founder, and head designer of Jupiter Fashion Agency, Edward almost certainly owns or heavily influences all key business decisions. This includes the website's user experience, the allocation of the marketing budget, and the selection of technology partners. He is the ultimate decision-maker responsible for the brand's growth, profitability, and digital strategy.
relevance_and_readiness.value_recognition: His experience at an enterprise-scale company like Forever 21 provides him with a clear understanding of the revenue impact of site performance. Now, as the leader of a lean, founder-led brand, he is uniquely prepared to appreciate Nostra's value proposition: achieving the kind of performance, crawl optimization, and bot protection that benefits a giant like Forever 21, but delivered in a way that is accessible and rapidly deployable for a smaller, agile company without a large engineering department.

recommended_approach.outreach_angle: The strategic angle is to lead with the shared context of Forever 21, using specific, quantified outcomes Nostra delivered to establish immediate credibility. This connection should then be directly translated to the challenges and opportunities he faces today as the founder of Jupiter Fashion Agency, positioning Nostra as the tool to achieve enterprise-grade results with founder-friendly efficiency.
recommended_approach.sample_subject_line: From F21 to Jupiter: a thought on site speed & revenue
recommended_approach.sample_message: Hi Edward—at F21 you saw firsthand how site speed and crawl budget directly impact revenue. Nostra’s Edge Delivery Engine and Crawler Optimization helped F21 cut TTFB by 53%, speed up pages by 59%, and increase crawl speed by 166%. We bring that same enterprise edge stack to founder-led brands like Jupiter in days, not months—delivering a faster mobile UX, cleaner SEO for better discovery, and bot filtering to protect your ROAS. Can I have 15 minutes to run a live speed test and a quick bot audit on your site? If we can’t show a meaningful path to lift your conversions, I’ll buy the coffee.

takeaway.verdict: This is a high-fit, high-urgency target: a founder with direct context from his time at Forever 21 who is now leading a lean fashion brand that can immediately monetize the performance, SEO, and bot-protection gains Nostra delivers, all without a heavy engineering lift.

outputBasis.reasoning: The most directly relevant content shows Edward Zenon in connection with Jupiter Fashion Agency as the current company and identifies Forever 21 as a past company in which he held a leadership role. Specifically, records indicate he is the creative team lead and head designer at Jupiter Fashion Agency, establishing his current authority over brand direction and digital strategy. Other excerpts confirm that Forever 21 was a past employer for him, reinforcing the alumni signal that his prior exposure to a major retailer’s site performance and e-commerce challenges would be known to him. Additional excerpts describe Forever 21’s experiences with optimization tools like Nostra AI, illustrating the practical outcomes of these performance initiatives in the context of the retailer Edward previously touched, thereby enriching the rationale for why he would recognize value in Nostra’s proposition. Taken together, these excerpts establish a coherent narrative: Edward’s past at Forever 21 provides experiential context for site performance and e-commerce optimization challenges; his present leadership at Jupiter Fashion Agency gives him the authority to influence digital infrastructure decisions; and the demonstrated impact at Forever 21 underscores why he would be receptive to a performance-focused edge solution like Nostra.

This supports the idea that the lead operates in a boutique fashion/creative services environment where brand storytelling and rapid iteration are important, which aligns with the need for fast, reliable digital infrastructure in the target memo. The second item, mentioning Jupiter Fashion Agency in association with Edward Zenon, reinforces the link to the target company and the alumni signal that the lead has exposure to or awareness of the client’s product through prior work at a customer company. However, the excerpts do not provide explicit details about Jupiter Fashion Agency’s scale, ecommerce footprint, or growth trajectory, so while they establish relevance, they offer limited direct evidence about the current market position or infrastructure urgency beyond inference from the fashion/creative agency context. Overall, the connection is solid for validating the target company and alumni signal, but supports only partial inference about the broader market_position factors; thus, the strength of the field value support is moderate.

citations.title: Site Speed Optimization: How to Speed up Your Site - Nostra
excerpts:
.nostra.ai/edge-delivery-engine) revolutionizes website performance by dramatically accelerating site speed within minutes, not months.  Our hybrid server-side rendering product enables smart caching mechanisms for more of your content—including dynamic content—and utilizes more than 310 edge locations, which are strategically located within 50 milliseconds of 97% of the world’s population.
By

By utilizing serverless functions on our edge servers, our software makes decisions before your pages load. This ensures that content is delivered to your users faster, typically improving measured page load times by 20-30% and reducing Time-to-First-Byte (TTFB) by 5-10 times.

The Edge Delivery Engine™ approach to improving load times through high-speed infrastructure and intelligent page speed optimizations elevates website performance metrics recognized by Google, positively impacting your SEO algorithm rankings and improving your overall customer experience.

outputBasis.reasoning: The core field value requires an outreach angle that foregrounds Forever 21 as a proven reference point and anchors it to founder-led brands like Jupiter Fashion Agency, with concrete, quantified outcomes. The most directly supportive excerpts provide exact metrics from Forever 21’s use of Nostra AI: reductions in Time to First Byte, substantial page-load speed improvements, and a massive crawl-speed uplift. These numbers are the backbone of a credible, time-saving, revenue-enhancing proposition that the outreach can promise to Edward Zenon, given his leadership role at Jupiter Fashion Agency and his past exposure to Forever 21’s digital performance journey. Additional excerpts document Forever 21’s strategic importance to Nostra’s client base and the enterprise-grade impact of the Edge Delivery Engine, reinforcing that the same playbook can be applied to a founder-led, growth-focused agency. Contextual pieces about Forever 21’s business relevance and the Nostra Edge Delivery Engine reinforce credibility, while excerpts about the alumni signal (Edward Zenon’s current and past affiliations) establish a clear bridge to the target. The combined evidence supports an outreach angle that opens with the Forever 21 success story, cites precise performance gains, and then translates those gains into a founder-friendly path for Jupiter Fashion Agency, emphasizing speed, SEO impact, bot protection, and revenue lift. The more general pieces about the Edge Delivery Engine ROI and site-speed optimization further back up the claim that rapid, measurable improvements are achievable, which strengthens the proposed talking points and the live-test invitation included in the sample message. Overall, the strongest support comes from the explicit Forever 21 performance numbers, followed by corroborating Forever 21/Nostra narratives and engine-level benefits, then alumni-contextual references to Edward Zenon and Jupiter Fashion Agency.

300+ enterprise and high-growth brands rely on Nostra to deliver instant site speed, stronger SEO performance, deeper personalization, and clean, actionable marketing data.

Nostra’s ROI is undeniable. Our entire site experience has always been very brand-focused, with rich full-bleed imagery and lots of content that many told us would slow down our site. citations.title: Site Speed Optimization: How to Speed up Your Site - Nostra
excerpts: By utilizing serverless functions on our edge servers, our software makes decisions before your pages load. This ensures that content is delivered to your users faster, typically improving measured page load times by 20-30% and reducing Time-to-First-Byte (TTFB) by 5-10 times.

The Edge Delivery Engine™ approach to improving load times through high-speed infrastructure and intelligent page speed optimizations elevates website performance metrics recognized by Google, positively impacting your SEO algorithm rankings and improving your overall customer experience.

lead_identity.description: A brief introduction to the lead, including their name and current role.

alumni_signal.description: A clear statement of the lead's connection to a past company that is a known customer of the client.

company_context.description: The current company's scale, growth stage, and ecommerce footprint.

competitive_pressure.description: The market pressures the company faces and what is at stake for them.

infrastructure_urgency.description: Why their digital infrastructure, site performance, and conversion optimization are critical right now.

past_experience.description: What the lead likely saw or was aware of regarding site performance and ecommerce challenges at their past company.

current_authority.description: What the lead owns or influences in their current role as CEO/Designer.

value_recognition.description: Why their past experience makes them uniquely ready to understand the client's value proposition.

outreach_angle.description: The specific strategic angle for the outreach, referencing the alumni connection and current situation.

sample_subject_line.description: A concrete, personalized subject line for an outreach email.

sample_message.description: A sample talk track or message body for the outreach.

verdict.description: The final verdict on why this lead is a high-value target.

urgency_level.description: The recommended urgency level for pursuing this lead (e.g., High, Medium, Low).
